question.text: What is the minimum record retention period under the IFSCA (AML, CFT and KYC) Guidelines for CDD documents and transaction records?

answer.text: At least six years from the date the business relationship ended or transaction was completed
At least five years from the date the business relationship ended
At least ten years from the date of account opening
At least eight years from the date the transaction was completed

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The Regulated Entity shall preserve all necessary records for at least six years, or for such period as prescribed under applicable laws, from the date on which the business relationship has ended or the transaction is completed.
**The Trap:** The six-year period starts from when the relationship ENDED or transaction COMPLETED — not from when the relationship started or the document was created.
**Cross-Exam Context:** In Series XXIV, SEBI mandates five years for domestic intermediaries. IFSCA-01 requires six years, aligned with FATF standards. Note the one-year difference — this is an exam favorite.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Six years is the IFSCA standard. If the date when the relationship ended is unclear, it may be taken as the date of the last transaction. Records can be kept electronically if readily accessible.
